domains.name: Strategy & Governance
domains.subtitle: Setting direction and ensuring accountability
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the enterprise's ability to define its purpose, set strategic direction, govern its operations, manage enterprise-wide performance, and ensure the organisation adapts and transforms to remain competitive. It is the domain where long-term intent is formulated, corporate accountability is established, and enterprise-wide coordination is orchestrated.
In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, this domain also governs how the blended workforce itself is designed and overseen — ensuring that the human/agent operating model is a deliberate strategic choice, not an emergent accident.

capabilities.name: Define the Business Concept and Long-Term Vision
capabilities.description: Establish the foundational purpose, mission, values, and long-term vision of the enterprise. This capability ensures the organisation has a clearly articulated reason for existing and a compelling picture of its future state.

processes.name: Develop and articulate the enterprise mission and purpose
processes.agentic_note: Human-led; agents may research peer missions for benchmarking

measurements.name: Mission Clarity Score
measurements.what: Employee understanding and alignment with enterprise mission
measurements.why: Unclear mission leads to strategic drift
measurements.how: Annual survey score (1-10) on mission comprehension

measurements.name: Mission Review Cycle Adherence
measurements.what: Whether mission review occurs within planned cadence
measurements.why: Ensures mission stays relevant as markets evolve
measurements.how: Binary (1=on schedule, 0=overdue) tracked quarterly

processes.name: Define and codify organisational values and principles
processes.agentic_note: Human-led; values require authentic human deliberation

measurements.name: Values Awareness Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of employees who can articulate core values
measurements.why: Values only drive behaviour when known and understood
measurements.how: Survey respondents correctly identifying values / Total respondents × 100

measurements.name: Values-Behaviour Alignment Score
measurements.what: Degree to which observed behaviours reflect stated values
measurements.why: Gap between stated and lived values erodes culture
measurements.how: 360-degree assessment average score on values alignment (1-10)

processes.name: Formulate the long-term vision and strategic ambition
processes.agentic_note: Human-led with agent scenario modelling and trend analysis

measurements.name: Vision Refresh Timeliness
measurements.what: Whether vision is reviewed and refreshed on schedule
measurements.why: Stale vision leads to strategic misalignment
measurements.how: Days since last vision review vs planned review cycle (variance in days)

measurements.name: Strategic Ambition Stretch Ratio
measurements.what: Ratio of vision targets to current performance baseline
measurements.why: Ensures vision is aspirational enough to drive transformation
measurements.how: Vision target metric / Current baseline metric

processes.name: Communicate vision and purpose across the enterprise
processes.agentic_note: Agents generate communications; humans approve and personalise

measurements.name: Vision Communication Reach
measurements.what: Percentage of workforce who received vision communications
measurements.why: Vision only drives alignment when broadly communicated
measurements.how: Employees reached / Total headcount × 100

measurements.name: Vision Comprehension Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of employees who can articulate the strategic vision
measurements.why: Receiving communications does not guarantee understanding
measurements.how: Pulse survey correct responses / Total respondents × 100

processes.name: Review and refresh the business concept periodically
processes.agentic_note: Agents monitor market shifts and trigger review cycles

measurements.name: Business Concept Review Rate
measurements.what: Whether periodic reviews are completed on schedule
measurements.why: Ensures the business concept remains relevant
measurements.how: Reviews completed on time / Reviews scheduled × 100

measurements.name: Market Signal Response Time
measurements.what: Days between significant market shift and triggered review
measurements.why: Slow response risks competitive disadvantage
measurements.how: Review trigger date − market event date (calendar days)

capabilities.name: Develop and Manage Enterprise Strategy
capabilities.description: Translate the long-term vision into a coherent strategic plan that defines where the enterprise will compete, how it will win, and what capabilities it requires. This includes environmental analysis, strategic option evaluation, and strategy documentation.

processes.name: Conduct environmental and market analysis
processes.agentic_note: Agent-heavy; agents scan, synthesise, and present; humans interpret

measurements.name: Environmental Scan Coverage
measurements.what: Number of external domains systematically analysed
measurements.why: Blind spots lead to strategic surprises
measurements.how: Domains covered (PESTLE) / 6 × 100

measurements.name: Insight Freshness
measurements.what: Age of the most recent market analysis deliverable
measurements.why: Stale analysis leads to outdated decisions
measurements.how: Current date − latest analysis date (calendar days)

processes.name: Assess internal capabilities and strategic readiness
processes.agentic_note: Agents map capabilities and gaps; humans validate and prioritise

measurements.name: Capability Gap Identification Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of strategic capabilities assessed for readiness
measurements.why: Unidentified gaps delay strategy execution
measurements.how: Capabilities assessed / Total strategic capabilities × 100

measurements.name: Readiness Assessment Cycle Time
measurements.what: Days to complete a capability readiness assessment
measurements.why: Faster assessments enable responsive planning
measurements.how: Assessment completion date − start date (business days)

processes.name: Identify and evaluate strategic options
processes.agentic_note: Agents model scenarios and outcomes; humans judge and decide

measurements.name: Strategic Options Evaluated
measurements.what: Number of distinct strategic options formally evaluated
measurements.why: Too few options increases risk of suboptimal strategy
measurements.how: Count of formally evaluated strategic options per cycle

measurements.name: Scenario Model Accuracy
measurements.what: Variance between scenario projections and actual outcomes
measurements.why: Accurate models improve decision confidence
measurements.how: |Projected − Actual| / Actual × 100

processes.name: Select and commit to strategic direction
processes.agentic_note: Human decision; agents provide decision-support artefacts

measurements.name: Strategic Decision Cycle Time
measurements.what: Days from option presentation to formal commitment
measurements.why: Delayed decisions create uncertainty
measurements.how: Commitment date − options presentation date (calendar days)

measurements.name: Stakeholder Alignment Score
measurements.what: Degree of leadership alignment on chosen direction
measurements.why: Misalignment cascades into execution dysfunction
measurements.how: Leadership survey agreement score (1-10)

processes.name: Document and formalise the strategic plan
processes.agentic_note: Agents draft; humans review, refine, and approve

measurements.name: Strategic Plan Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of strategic plan sections fully documented
measurements.why: Incomplete plans lead to ambiguous execution
measurements.how: Completed sections / Required sections × 100

measurements.name: Plan Formalisation Cycle Time
measurements.what: Days from strategic decision to approved plan
measurements.why: Delays slow cascade and execution start
measurements.how: Plan approval date − decision date (business days)

processes.name: Cascade strategy across organisational units and domains
processes.agentic_note: Agents distribute and track acknowledgement; humans contextualise for their teams

measurements.name: Strategy Cascade Completion
measurements.what: Percentage of OUs with localised strategic plans
measurements.why: Uncascaded strategy remains executive abstraction
measurements.how: OUs with localised plans / Total OUs × 100

measurements.name: Cascade Acknowledgement Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of leaders confirming understanding
measurements.why: Acknowledgement is minimum bar for cascade effectiveness
measurements.how: Leaders confirming / Total unit leaders × 100

processes.name: Monitor strategic assumptions and trigger strategy refresh
processes.agentic_note: Agents continuously monitor assumptions; alert humans when conditions change

measurements.name: Assumption Monitoring Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of assumptions with active monitoring
measurements.why: Unmonitored assumptions become invisible risks
measurements.how: Assumptions monitored / Total assumptions × 100

measurements.name: Assumption Breach Alert Latency
measurements.what: Time between breach and alert to leadership
measurements.why: Faster alerts enable faster strategic response
measurements.how: Alert timestamp − breach detection timestamp (hours)

capabilities.name: Manage Strategic Planning Cycles and Cadences
capabilities.description: Define and operate the rhythms through which strategy is planned, reviewed, and refreshed — including annual planning cycles, quarterly reviews, and event-driven strategic responses.

processes.name: Define the strategic planning calendar and milestones
processes.agentic_note: Humans set cadences; agents schedule and track

measurements.name: Planning Calendar Adherence
measurements.what: Percentage of milestones completed on schedule
measurements.why: Missed milestones cascade delays
measurements.how: Milestones on time / Total milestones × 100

measurements.name: Planning Cycle Duration
measurements.what: Total elapsed days for annual planning cycle
measurements.why: Long cycles reduce agility
measurements.how: Cycle end date − start date (calendar days)

processes.name: Coordinate cross-functional input to strategic planning
processes.agentic_note: Agents orchestrate collection; humans contribute substance

measurements.name: Cross-Functional Input Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of functions providing input on time
measurements.why: Missing inputs create planning blind spots
measurements.how: Functions on time / Functions requested × 100

measurements.name: Input Quality Score
measurements.what: Average quality rating of cross-functional contributions
measurements.why: Low-quality inputs degrade plan quality
measurements.how: Average quality rating from reviewers (1-10)

processes.name: Facilitate strategic review sessions
processes.agentic_note: Agents prepare briefing packs; humans facilitate discussion

measurements.name: Review Session Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of scheduled reviews actually held
measurements.why: Skipped reviews reduce oversight
measurements.how: Reviews held / Reviews scheduled × 100

measurements.name: Review Action Closure Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of review actions completed by due date
measurements.why: Open items undermine review effectiveness
measurements.how: Actions closed on time / Total actions × 100

processes.name: Manage the transition from planning to execution
processes.agentic_note: Agents translate strategy into initiative structures; humans approve

measurements.name: Strategy-to-Initiative Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of objectives linked to funded initiatives
measurements.why: Untranslated strategies never reach execution
measurements.how: Objectives with initiatives / Total objectives × 100

measurements.name: Execution Readiness Score
measurements.what: Average readiness of initiatives entering execution
measurements.why: Poorly prepared initiatives fail during delivery
measurements.how: Average readiness checklist score (0-100%)

processes.name: Operate concurrent planning horizons
processes.agentic_note: Agents maintain multiple planning timelines; humans prioritise across horizons

measurements.name: Planning Horizon Coverage
measurements.what: Number of concurrent planning horizons maintained
measurements.why: Single-horizon planning misses long-term threats
measurements.how: Count of actively maintained planning horizons

measurements.name: Cross-Horizon Alignment Score
measurements.what: Consistency across short, medium, and long-term plans
measurements.why: Misaligned horizons create conflicting priorities
measurements.how: Planning review assessment score (1-10)

capabilities.name: Manage Corporate Governance and Board Operations
capabilities.description: Ensure the enterprise operates within its governance framework — including board management, fiduciary oversight, shareholder relations, and regulatory governance obligations.

processes.name: Manage board composition, meetings, and administration
processes.agentic_note: Agents handle scheduling, document prep, minutes; humans govern

measurements.name: Board Meeting Adherence
measurements.what: Percentage of board meetings held on schedule
measurements.why: Missed meetings impair governance oversight
measurements.how: Meetings on schedule / Meetings planned × 100

measurements.name: Board Pack Timeliness
measurements.what: Days before meeting that packs are distributed
measurements.why: Late distribution reduces preparation quality
measurements.how: Meeting date − pack distribution date (calendar days)

processes.name: Prepare and distribute board and committee packs
processes.agentic_note: Agent-assembled; humans review and approve content

measurements.name: Board Pack Completeness
measurements.what: Percentage of required sections included
measurements.why: Incomplete packs impair decision-making
measurements.how: Sections included / Required sections × 100

measurements.name: Pack Preparation Cycle Time
measurements.what: Days to assemble and approve the board pack
measurements.why: Shorter cycles allow more current information
measurements.how: Approval date − assembly start date (business days)

processes.name: Manage fiduciary and regulatory governance obligations
processes.agentic_note: Agents monitor compliance calendars; humans exercise fiduciary judgement

measurements.name: Governance Compliance Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of obligations met on time
measurements.why: Non-compliance creates legal and reputational risk
measurements.how: Obligations met on time / Total obligations × 100

measurements.name: Regulatory Filing Timeliness
measurements.what: Average days before deadline that filings are submitted
measurements.why: Cutting it close increases missed-deadline risk
measurements.how: Average (deadline − submission date) across filings (days)

processes.name: Coordinate shareholder and stakeholder communications
processes.agentic_note: Agents draft; humans approve tone and content

measurements.name: Stakeholder Comms Timeliness
measurements.what: Percentage of communications delivered on schedule
measurements.why: Late communications erode confidence
measurements.how: Communications on time / Planned communications × 100

measurements.name: Stakeholder Satisfaction Score
measurements.what: Satisfaction with quality and transparency
measurements.why: Low satisfaction signals trust gaps
measurements.how: Average stakeholder survey score (1-10)

processes.name: Maintain the corporate governance framework and policies
processes.agentic_note: Agents track regulatory changes and flag policy gaps; humans update policies

measurements.name: Policy Currency Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of policies reviewed within cycle
measurements.why: Outdated policies create compliance gaps
measurements.how: Policies reviewed on schedule / Total policies × 100

measurements.name: Policy Gap Closure Time
measurements.what: Average days to close identified policy gaps
measurements.why: Open gaps represent governance exposure
measurements.how: Average gap closure date − identification date (calendar days)

capabilities.name: Manage Enterprise Performance
capabilities.description: Establish and operate the enterprise performance management system — defining KPIs, tracking outcomes, conducting performance reviews, and ensuring accountability from strategy through to execution.

processes.name: Define enterprise KPIs and performance frameworks
processes.agentic_note: Humans define what matters; agents benchmark and recommend targets

measurements.name: KPI Definition Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of objectives with defined KPIs
measurements.why: Unmeasured objectives cannot be managed
measurements.how: Objectives with KPIs / Total objectives × 100

measurements.name: KPI Target Setting Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of KPIs with approved targets
measurements.why: KPIs without targets provide data but not accountability
measurements.how: KPIs with targets / Total KPIs × 100

processes.name: Collect, aggregate, and validate performance data
processes.agentic_note: Agent-executed; continuous automated data collection

measurements.name: Data Collection Automation Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of KPIs with automated collection
measurements.why: Manual collection is slow and error-prone
measurements.how: KPIs with automated feeds / Total KPIs × 100

measurements.name: Data Freshness
measurements.what: Average lag between event and data availability
measurements.why: Stale data delays decisions
measurements.how: Average (data availability − event timestamp) in hours

processes.name: Analyse performance trends and identify variances
processes.agentic_note: Agents analyse and surface insights; humans interpret meaning

measurements.name: Variance Detection Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of variances identified proactively
measurements.why: Proactive detection enables faster correction
measurements.how: Variances detected proactively / Total variances × 100

measurements.name: Trend Analysis Timeliness
measurements.what: Days after period close that analysis is available
measurements.why: Late analysis reduces actionability
measurements.how: Analysis date − period close date (business days)

processes.name: Conduct periodic performance reviews
processes.agentic_note: Agents prepare review materials; humans lead review discussions

measurements.name: Review Cadence Adherence
measurements.what: Percentage of reviews completed on time
measurements.why: Skipped reviews reduce accountability
measurements.how: Reviews on schedule / Reviews planned × 100

measurements.name: Review Decision Cycle Time
measurements.what: Days from review to documented decisions
measurements.why: Slow follow-through undermines review value
measurements.how: Decision date − review date (business days)

processes.name: Manage corrective actions and performance interventions
processes.agentic_note: Humans decide interventions; agents track execution and follow-up

measurements.name: Corrective Action Completion
measurements.what: Percentage of actions completed by target date
measurements.why: Incomplete actions allow issues to persist
measurements.how: Actions on time / Total corrective actions × 100

measurements.name: Intervention Effectiveness Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of interventions achieving target improvement
measurements.why: Ineffective interventions waste resources
measurements.how: Interventions achieving target / Total interventions × 100

processes.name: Report performance to governance bodies and stakeholders
processes.agentic_note: Agents generate reports; humans present and contextualise

measurements.name: Performance Report Timeliness
measurements.what: Days after period close that reports are delivered
measurements.why: Late reports reduce oversight quality
measurements.how: Report date − period close date (business days)

measurements.name: Report Accuracy Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of reports needing no corrections
measurements.why: Corrections erode reporting credibility
measurements.how: Reports without corrections / Total reports × 100

capabilities.name: Manage Strategic Partnerships and Alliances
capabilities.description: Identify, evaluate, establish, and govern strategic partnerships, joint ventures, alliances, and ecosystem relationships that extend the enterprise's capabilities and market reach.

processes.name: Identify and evaluate potential strategic partners
processes.agentic_note: Agents scan market and assess fit; humans evaluate relationship potential

measurements.name: Partner Pipeline Volume
measurements.what: Number of potential partners under evaluation
measurements.why: Healthy pipeline ensures future partnerships
measurements.how: Count of partners in evaluation pipeline

measurements.name: Partner Evaluation Cycle Time
measurements.what: Days from identification to go/no-go decision
measurements.why: Slow evaluation delays partnership benefits
measurements.how: Decision date − identification date (calendar days)

processes.name: Negotiate and formalise partnership agreements
processes.agentic_note: Human-led negotiation; agents support with research and document preparation

measurements.name: Partnership Agreement Cycle Time
measurements.what: Days from negotiation start to signed agreement
measurements.why: Protracted negotiations delay value
measurements.how: Signature date − negotiation start date (calendar days)

measurements.name: Partnership Terms Achievement
measurements.what: Percentage of priority terms in final agreement
measurements.why: Ensures agreements protect enterprise interests
measurements.how: Required terms included / Total required terms × 100

processes.name: Manage ongoing partner relationships and governance
processes.agentic_note: Humans own relationships; agents track commitments and performance

measurements.name: Partner Satisfaction Score
measurements.what: Partner-reported satisfaction with the relationship
measurements.why: Dissatisfied partners disengage
measurements.how: Average partner survey score (1-10)

measurements.name: Commitment Fulfilment Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of mutual commitments fulfilled on time
measurements.why: Unfulfilled commitments erode trust
measurements.how: Commitments on time / Total commitments × 100

processes.name: Measure partner value and contribution
processes.agentic_note: Agents track metrics and ROI; humans assess strategic value

measurements.name: Partner ROI
measurements.what: Return on investment from strategic partnerships
measurements.why: Ensures partnerships deliver value
measurements.how: Value generated / Investment in partnership × 100

measurements.name: Value Assessment Completion
measurements.what: Percentage of partners with completed value assessments
measurements.why: Unassessed partnerships may not return value
measurements.how: Assessed partners / Total active partners × 100

processes.name: Manage partner ecosystem coordination
processes.agentic_note: Agents orchestrate information flow; humans manage strategic alignment

measurements.name: Ecosystem Coordination Score
measurements.what: Rating of cross-partner coordination quality
measurements.why: Poor coordination reduces ecosystem value
measurements.how: Partner survey coordination score (1-10)

measurements.name: Information Sharing Timeliness
measurements.what: Average time for critical info to reach partners
measurements.why: Delayed sharing reduces responsiveness
measurements.how: Average distribution time for priority comms (hours)

capabilities.name: Manage Enterprise Architecture and Operating Model
capabilities.description: Define, maintain, and evolve the enterprise's architecture and operating model — including the structural hierarchy, domain configuration, and blended workforce design that ZORBA itself describes.

processes.name: Define and maintain the enterprise operating model
processes.agentic_note: Humans design; agents maintain documentation and track drift

measurements.name: Operating Model Currency
measurements.what: Whether documentation is current
measurements.why: Outdated docs lead to operating model drift
measurements.how: Days since last operating model review

measurements.name: Operating Model Drift Score
measurements.what: Divergence between documented and actual model
measurements.why: Drift indicates governance gaps
measurements.how: Count of undocumented deviations from audit

processes.name: Manage the enterprise domain and capability map
processes.agentic_note: Agents maintain the living map; humans make structural decisions

measurements.name: Domain Map Completeness
measurements.what: Percentage of capabilities mapped and documented
measurements.why: Gaps create strategic blind spots
measurements.how: Capabilities documented / Total capabilities × 100

measurements.name: Capability Map Update Frequency
measurements.what: Average days between capability map updates
measurements.why: Infrequent updates reduce relevance
measurements.how: Average days between consecutive map updates

processes.name: Design and govern the blended workforce architecture
processes.agentic_note: Humans set workforce composition targets; agents model scenarios and monitor actuals

measurements.name: Blended Workforce Maturity
measurements.what: Maturity of the human-agent workforce architecture
measurements.why: Immature design leads to suboptimal agent deployment
measurements.how: Maturity score against framework benchmarks (1-5)

measurements.name: Workforce Composition Adherence
measurements.what: Deviation from planned human-agent ratios
measurements.why: Large deviations indicate poor workforce planning
measurements.how: |Actual ratio − Target ratio| / Target ratio × 100

processes.name: Manage framework adoption and customisation
processes.agentic_note: Agents track framework versions and compliance; humans decide adaptations

measurements.name: Framework Adoption Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of OUs using the adopted framework
measurements.why: Partial adoption reduces framework value
measurements.how: OUs using framework / Applicable OUs × 100

measurements.name: Framework Version Currency
measurements.what: Whether enterprise uses current framework version
measurements.why: Outdated versions miss improvements
measurements.how: Current version / Latest available version

processes.name: Conduct operating model reviews and maturity assessments
processes.agentic_note: Agents assess maturity against benchmarks; humans interpret and act

measurements.name: Operating Model Maturity Score
measurements.what: Overall maturity from latest assessment
measurements.why: Tracks operating model sophistication
measurements.how: Maturity assessment aggregate score (1-5)

measurements.name: Recommendation Closure Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of assessment recommendations implemented
measurements.why: Unimplemented recommendations indicate stalled improvement
measurements.how: Implemented / Total recommendations × 100

capabilities.name: Manage Business Transformation and Change
capabilities.description: Plan, coordinate, and execute enterprise-wide transformation initiatives — including organisational change, digital transformation, operating model transitions, and strategic restructuring.

processes.name: Identify and prioritise transformation opportunities
processes.agentic_note: Agents surface opportunities from data; humans prioritise by strategic value

measurements.name: Transformation Pipeline Volume
measurements.what: Number of opportunities under evaluation
measurements.why: Healthy pipeline ensures improvement momentum
measurements.how: Count of opportunities in evaluation

measurements.name: Prioritisation Cycle Time
measurements.what: Days from identification to prioritisation decision
measurements.why: Slow prioritisation delays value
measurements.how: Decision date − identification date (calendar days)

processes.name: Design transformation programmes and roadmaps
processes.agentic_note: Humans design; agents model dependencies and timelines

measurements.name: Roadmap Completeness Score
measurements.what: Percentage of programmes with complete roadmaps
measurements.why: Incomplete roadmaps cause execution confusion
measurements.how: Programmes with complete roadmaps / Total programmes × 100

measurements.name: Programme Design Cycle Time
measurements.what: Days from approval to completed roadmap
measurements.why: Slow design delays execution
measurements.how: Roadmap date − approval date (business days)

processes.name: Manage organisational change and stakeholder readiness
processes.agentic_note: Humans lead change; agents monitor sentiment and readiness indicators

measurements.name: Change Readiness Score
measurements.what: Organisational readiness for active transformations
measurements.why: Low readiness predicts change failure
measurements.how: Average readiness survey score (1-10)

measurements.name: Stakeholder Engagement Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of stakeholders actively engaged in change
measurements.why: Disengaged stakeholders become blockers
measurements.how: Participating / Total identified stakeholders × 100

processes.name: Execute and track transformation initiatives
processes.agentic_note: Agents track milestones, risks, and dependencies; humans lead delivery

measurements.name: Milestone Adherence Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of milestones achieved on schedule
measurements.why: Missed milestones signal programme issues
measurements.how: Milestones on time / Total milestones × 100

measurements.name: Transformation Budget Variance
measurements.what: Percentage deviation from approved budget
measurements.why: Budget overruns threaten viability
measurements.how: (Actual − Budget) / Budget × 100

processes.name: Measure transformation outcomes and realise benefits
processes.agentic_note: Agents measure and report; humans validate benefit realisation

measurements.name: Benefit Realisation Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of planned benefits actually realised
measurements.why: Unrealised benefits represent wasted investment
measurements.how: Benefits realised / Benefits planned × 100

measurements.name: Outcome Measurement Timeliness
measurements.what: Days after milestone to completed measurement
measurements.why: Delayed measurement reduces course-correction ability
measurements.how: Measurement date − milestone date (business days)

processes.name: Embed change and ensure sustainability of new ways of working
processes.agentic_note: Humans reinforce behaviours; agents monitor adoption and flag regression

measurements.name: New Process Adoption Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of target population using new processes
measurements.why: Low adoption means transformation hasn't stuck
measurements.how: Users on new process / Target population × 100

measurements.name: Regression Incident Count
measurements.what: Instances of teams reverting to old ways
measurements.why: Regressions indicate insufficient embedding
measurements.how: Count of documented regression incidents

capabilities.name: Manage Enterprise Risk Oversight
capabilities.description: Provide enterprise-wide risk oversight — identifying, assessing, and monitoring strategic and operational risks that span domains and organisational units. This is the strategic risk layer; domain-specific risk management sits within Domain 11 (Risk, Legal & Compliance).

processes.name: Define the enterprise risk appetite and tolerance
processes.agentic_note: Human decision; agents provide risk landscape context

measurements.name: Risk Appetite Currency
measurements.what: Whether risk appetite is reviewed within cycle
measurements.why: Outdated appetite may not reflect current context
measurements.how: Days since last board-approved review

measurements.name: Risk Appetite Awareness
measurements.what: Percentage of leaders who can articulate risk appetite
measurements.why: Unknown appetite leads to inconsistent risk-taking
measurements.how: Leaders correctly describing / Total senior leaders × 100

processes.name: Identify and assess enterprise-level risks
processes.agentic_note: Agents scan for emerging risks; humans assess severity and likelihood

measurements.name: Risk Register Completeness
measurements.what: Percentage of risk categories with assessed risks
measurements.why: Gaps represent unmanaged exposure
measurements.how: Categories with risks / Total categories × 100

measurements.name: Risk Assessment Refresh Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of risks reassessed within review cycle
measurements.why: Stale assessments may not reflect current levels
measurements.how: Risks reassessed on schedule / Total risks × 100

processes.name: Monitor the enterprise risk landscape continuously
processes.agentic_note: Agent-executed; continuous monitoring with human-defined alert thresholds

measurements.name: Risk Monitoring Uptime
measurements.what: Percentage of time monitoring is operational
measurements.why: Downtime creates blind spots
measurements.how: Uptime hours / Total hours × 100

measurements.name: Emerging Risk Detection Rate
measurements.what: Number of emerging risks identified per quarter
measurements.why: Active monitoring should surface new risks
measurements.how: Count of new risks from monitoring systems

processes.name: Report risk exposure to governance bodies
processes.agentic_note: Agents compile risk reports; humans present and contextualise

measurements.name: Risk Report Timeliness
measurements.what: Days before meeting that risk reports are delivered
measurements.why: Late reports reduce oversight quality
measurements.how: Meeting date − report delivery date (calendar days)

measurements.name: Risk Report Completeness
measurements.what: Percentage of required dimensions covered
measurements.why: Incomplete reporting creates blind spots
measurements.how: Dimensions covered / Required dimensions × 100

processes.name: Coordinate risk response across domains and OUs
processes.agentic_note: Humans lead cross-domain response; agents track actions and dependencies

measurements.name: Cross-Domain Response Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of multi-domain risks with coordinated plans
measurements.why: Uncoordinated responses reduce effectiveness
measurements.how: Risks with coordinated plans / Total cross-domain risks × 100

measurements.name: Risk Action Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of risk actions completed by target date
measurements.why: Incomplete actions leave residual exposure
measurements.how: Actions on time / Total risk actions × 100

capabilities.name: Manage Mergers, Acquisitions, and Divestitures
capabilities.description: Evaluate, execute, and integrate (or separate) corporate transactions that reshape the enterprise's portfolio of businesses.

processes.name: Scan and evaluate acquisition and divestiture targets
processes.agentic_note: Agents screen and model; humans evaluate strategic fit

measurements.name: M&A Target Pipeline
measurements.what: Number of targets under active evaluation
measurements.why: Healthy pipeline supports portfolio management
measurements.how: Count of targets in active evaluation

measurements.name: Target Screening Efficiency
measurements.what: Ratio of targets advancing to due diligence
measurements.why: Indicates screening criteria quality
measurements.how: Targets advancing / Targets screened × 100

processes.name: Conduct due diligence
processes.agentic_note: Agents process and analyse data rooms; humans assess risks and red flags

measurements.name: Due Diligence Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of workstreams completed on schedule
measurements.why: Incomplete diligence increases transaction risk
measurements.how: Workstreams on time / Total workstreams × 100

measurements.name: Critical Issue Discovery Rate
measurements.what: Material issues discovered during due diligence
measurements.why: Effective diligence surfaces deal-breakers before close

domains.name: Business Development & Client Acquisition
domains.subtitle: Winning work through relationships, proposals, and trusted advisory
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the firm's ability to identify, pursue, and win client engagements. In professional services, business development is relationship-led and proposal-driven rather than transactional. Win strategies centre on demonstrating domain expertise, assembling the right team, and navigating complex procurement processes including RFPs, framework agreements, and competitive tenders.
In a ZORBA-enabled professional services firm, agents handle proposal assembly, competitive intelligence gathering, CRM hygiene, and bid coordination, while humans focus on client relationships, solution shaping, orals presentations, and commercial negotiation.

domains.name: Client Engagement & Delivery Excellence
domains.subtitle: Delivering outcomes and building lasting client partnerships
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the firm's ability to deliver exceptional client outcomes and build enduring advisory relationships. In professional services, client success is inseparable from delivery — satisfaction is earned through engagement execution, not post-sale support functions. The focus is on delivery quality, stakeholder management, scope governance, and turning successful engagements into long-term partnerships.
In a ZORBA-enabled professional services firm, agents monitor engagement health metrics, automate status reporting, track deliverable completion, and flag risks early, while humans manage client relationships, navigate organisational politics, and provide the expert judgement that defines advisory value.

capabilities.name: Manage Customer Support and Issue Resolution

processes.name: Receive, classify, and route customer inquiries

measurements.name: Inquiry Classification Accuracy
measurements.what: Percentage of inquiries correctly classified and routed on first attempt

measurements.how: Correctly routed inquiries / Total inquiries × 100

measurements.name: Average Routing Time
measurements.what: Average time from inquiry receipt to assignment to resolving agent or team

measurements.how: Sum of (assignment time − receipt time) / Total inquiries

processes.name: Resolve standard issues through automated self-service

measurements.name: Self-Service Deflection Rate

domains.name: Engagement Delivery & Project Management
domains.subtitle: Managing the lifecycle of client engagements from mobilisation to close
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the firm's ability to plan, execute, and close client engagements effectively. In professional services, operations *is* delivery — project and programme management, resource scheduling, utilisation tracking, milestone management, and change control are the operational core of the business.
In a ZORBA-enabled professional services firm, agents handle resource scheduling optimisation, timesheet analysis, milestone tracking, budget burn-rate monitoring, and automated status consolidation, while humans lead engagement governance, client steering committees, risk escalation, and the creative problem-solving that defines high-value delivery.

capabilities.name: Plan and Schedule Operational Activities
capabilities.description: Translate demand signals and strategic priorities into executable operational plans and schedules that balance throughput, resource constraints, and customer commitments.

processes.name: Develop aggregate operational plans aligned to demand
processes.agentic_note: Agents model demand scenarios; humans select and approve plans

measurements.name: Plan-to-Demand Alignment
measurements.what: Percentage variance between operational plan output and actual demand
measurements.why: Misaligned plans lead to over/under production and wasted resources
measurements.how: (Planned output − Actual demand) / Actual demand × 100

measurements.name: Planning Cycle Time
measurements.what: Days to complete the aggregate operational planning cycle
measurements.why: Faster planning enables more responsive operations
measurements.how: Plan approval date − planning start date (business days)

processes.name: Create and maintain detailed production or service schedules
processes.agentic_note: Agents generate optimised schedules; humans review constraints and approve

measurements.name: Schedule Adherence Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of scheduled activities completed as planned
measurements.why: Schedule adherence drives on-time delivery and resource efficiency

domains.name: Subcontractor & Vendor Management
domains.subtitle: Managing the extended workforce and specialist vendor ecosystem
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the firm's ability to source, manage, and govern its extended workforce of subcontractors, associate consultants, specialist vendors, and technology partners. Professional services firms do not operate physical supply chains — their "supply" is people and specialist capabilities sourced from an ecosystem of trusted partners and independents.
In a ZORBA-enabled professional services firm, agents manage subcontractor databases, rate card compliance, availability tracking, IR35/contractor compliance checks, and performance scoring, while humans manage strategic partner relationships, negotiate framework agreements, and make staffing decisions for client engagements.

measurements.what: Improvement in forecast accuracy from adding market/sales intelligence
measurements.why: Validates the value of human judgement in forecasting
measurements.how: Statistical MAPE − Enriched MAPE

measurements.name: Intelligence Integration Timeliness
measurements.what: Percentage of forecast cycles with market intelligence integrated on time
measurements.why: Late intelligence reduces its value in planning decisions
measurements.how: Cycles with timely integration / Total cycles × 100

processes.name: Conduct sales and operations planning cycles
processes.agentic_note: Humans lead consensus process; agents prepare scenarios and gap analysis

measurements.name: S&OP Cycle Adherence
measurements.what: Percentage of S&OP cycles completed within scheduled timeframe
measurements.why: Disciplined cadence ensures plans stay current
measurements.how: On-time S&OP cycles / Total S&OP cycles × 100

measurements.name: Demand-Supply Gap Resolution Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of identified demand-supply gaps resolved during S&OP
measurements.why: Unresolved gaps cascade into service failures
measurements.how: Gaps resolved / Total gaps identified × 100

processes.name: Translate demand plans into supply and production signals
processes.agentic_note: Agents generate supply plans; humans validate feasibility

measurements.name: Plan Translation Accuracy
measurements.what: Accuracy of supply/production signals vs original demand plan intent
measurements.why: Translation errors compound through the supply chain
measurements.how: 1 − |Supply signal − Demand plan| / Demand plan × 100

measurements.name: Signal Generation Timeliness
measurements.what: Percentage of supply signals generated within target lead time
measurements.why: Late signals constrain supplier and production response options
measurements.how: Timely signals / Total signals generated × 100

processes.name: Monitor forecast accuracy and refine models
processes.agentic_note: Agent-executed; continuous model tuning with human review of significant shifts

measurements.name: Model Accuracy Improvement Rate
measurements.what: Period-over-period improvement in forecast model accuracy
measurements.why: Continuous improvement validates model tuning effectiveness
measurements.how: Previous MAPE − Current MAPE

measurements.name: Model Refresh Frequency
measurements.what: Number of model retraining cycles completed per period
measurements.why: Regular retraining keeps models aligned with changing patterns
measurements.how: Count of model retraining cycles per quarter

processes.name: Manage demand shaping and demand sensing
processes.agentic_note: Agents detect demand signals in real time; humans decide shaping actions

measurements.name: Demand Signal Detection Latency
measurements.what: Average time from demand shift occurrence to detection
measurements.why: Faster sensing enables more responsive supply chain actions
measurements.how: Average (detection time − demand shift onset) in hours

measurements.name: Demand Shaping Effectiveness
measurements.what: Percentage of demand shaping actions achieving target demand shift
measurements.why: Effective shaping reduces supply chain stress
measurements.how: Successful shaping actions / Total shaping actions × 100

capabilities.name: Manage Supply Chain Risk and Resilience
capabilities.description: Identify, monitor, and mitigate risks across the extended supply chain — from single-source dependencies to geopolitical disruptions — ensuring the supply network can absorb and recover from shocks.

processes.name: Map and assess supply chain risk exposure
processes.agentic_note: Agents map multi-tier dependencies; humans assess strategic vulnerabilities

measurements.name: Supply Chain Risk Map Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of supply chain tiers with documented risk mapping
measurements.why: Unmapped tiers contain hidden vulnerabilities
measurements.how: Mapped tiers / Total known tiers × 100

measurements.name: Risk Assessment Currency
measurements.what: Percentage of risk assessments updated within their review cycle
measurements.why: Stale assessments underrepresent current risk exposure
measurements.how: Current assessments / Total assessments × 100

processes.name: Monitor supply chain risk signals and early warnings
processes.agentic_note: Agent-executed; continuous scanning of geopolitical, weather, and financial signals

measurements.name: Early Warning Detection Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of supply chain disruptions detected before operational impact
measurements.why: Early detection enables proactive mitigation
measurements.how: Pre-impact detections / Total disruptions × 100

measurements.name: Signal Monitoring Breadth
measurements.what: Number of distinct risk signal sources actively monitored
measurements.why: Broader monitoring reduces blind spots
measurements.how: Count of active risk signal sources

processes.name: Develop and maintain supply chain contingency plans
processes.agentic_note: Humans design contingencies; agents maintain playbooks and test readiness

measurements.name: Contingency Plan Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of critical supply paths with documented contingency plans
measurements.why: Unplanned disruptions without contingencies cause extended outages
measurements.how: Paths with plans / Total critical paths × 100

measurements.name: Contingency Plan Test Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of contingency plans tested within their review cycle
measurements.why: Untested plans may not work when activated
measurements.how: Plans tested on schedule / Total plans × 100

processes.name: Execute supply chain disruption response
processes.agentic_note: Agents activate playbooks and reroute; humans manage critical decisions

measurements.name: Disruption Response Time
measurements.what: Average time from disruption detection to response activation
measurements.why: Faster response limits disruption impact
measurements.how: Average (response activation − detection) in hours

measurements.name: Disruption Recovery Time
measurements.what: Average time from disruption to return to normal operations
measurements.why: Shorter recovery reduces revenue and service impact
measurements.how: Average (normal operations restored − disruption start) in days

processes.name: Conduct supply chain resilience assessments and stress tests
processes.agentic_note: Agents run simulations; humans evaluate findings and invest in resilience

measurements.name: Stress Test Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of planned resilience stress tests completed
measurements.why: Incomplete testing leaves gaps in preparedness understanding
measurements.how: Tests completed / Tests planned × 100

measurements.name: Resilience Score
measurements.what: Composite score measuring supply chain ability to absorb and recover from disruptions
measurements.why: Holistic measure of supply chain robustness
measurements.how: Weighted composite of redundancy, flexibility, visibility, and recovery metrics (0-100)

capabilities.name: Govern Third-Party and Partner Ecosystem Relationships
capabilities.description: Manage the broader ecosystem of third-party service providers, logistics partners, contract manufacturers, and platform participants that extend the enterprise's supply chain capabilities.

processes.name: Define third-party governance frameworks and standards
processes.agentic_note: Humans set governance standards; agents benchmark and track compliance

measurements.name: Governance Framework Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of third-party relationships with defined governance standards
measurements.why: Ungoverned relationships increase risk and inconsistency
measurements.how: Relationships with governance / Total third-party relationships × 100

measurements.name: Governance Compliance Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of partners meeting governance standards
measurements.why: Non-compliance undermines the governance framework
measurements.how: Compliant partners / Total governed partners × 100

processes.name: Onboard and integrate ecosystem partners
processes.agentic_note: Agents manage onboarding workflows; humans approve and establish relationships

measurements.name: Partner Onboarding Cycle Time
measurements.what: Average days from partner approval to operational integration
measurements.why: Faster onboarding accelerates ecosystem value
measurements.how: Average (integration complete − approval date) in days

measurements.name: Integration Success Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of partner integrations completed without critical issues
measurements.why: Failed integrations delay value realisation and damage relationships
measurements.how: Successful integrations / Total integrations × 100

processes.name: Monitor ecosystem partner performance and compliance
processes.agentic_note: Agents track SLAs and compliance metrics; humans manage escalations

measurements.name: Partner SLA Compliance Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of ecosystem partners meeting their SLA commitments
measurements.why: Partner performance directly impacts enterprise delivery
measurements.how: Partners meeting SLAs / Total partners with SLAs × 100

measurements.name: Partner Compliance Audit Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of partners audited within scheduled cycle
measurements.why: Regular audits ensure ongoing compliance
measurements.how: Partners audited on schedule / Total partners due × 100

processes.name: Manage data exchange and integration across the ecosystem
processes.agentic_note: Agents operate integration platforms; humans govern data standards

measurements.name: Data Exchange Reliability
measurements.what: Percentage of data exchanges completed successfully without errors
measurements.why: Reliable data exchange is the foundation of ecosystem collaboration
measurements.how: Successful exchanges / Total exchanges × 100

measurements.name: Integration Uptime
measurements.what: Percentage of time ecosystem integration platforms are operational
measurements.why: Downtime disrupts partner collaboration and operations
measurements.how: (Total time − Downtime) / Total time × 100

processes.name: Review and rationalise the partner ecosystem periodically
processes.agentic_note: Humans make portfolio decisions; agents analyse utilisation and overlap

measurements.name: Ecosystem Rationalisation Savings
measurements.what: Cost savings achieved through partner portfolio rationalisation
measurements.why: Eliminates redundancy and focuses investment on high-value partners
measurements.how: Cost reduction from rationalised partners per year

measurements.name: Partner Portfolio Review Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of scheduled ecosystem reviews completed on time
measurements.why: Regular reviews ensure the portfolio stays aligned with strategy
measurements.how: Reviews completed / Reviews scheduled × 100

capabilities.name: Manage Subcontractor Sourcing and Onboarding
capabilities.description: Identify, vet, and onboard subcontractors and associate consultants into the firm's extended workforce pool. Ensure compliance with IR35, right-to-work, insurance, and confidentiality requirements.

processes.name: Maintain the approved subcontractor and associate database
processes.agentic_note: Agents manage database, flag expiring certifications; humans approve additions

processes.name: Conduct subcontractor vetting and compliance checks
processes.agentic_note: Agents automate background checks, IR35 assessments; humans review edge cases

processes.name: Negotiate subcontractor rate cards and framework terms
processes.agentic_note: Human-led commercial negotiation; agents provide market rate benchmarks

processes.name: Manage subcontractor onboarding and system access provisioning
processes.agentic_note: Agents orchestrate onboarding workflows; humans approve access and assignments

capabilities.name: Track Subcontractor Utilisation and Availability
capabilities.description: Monitor the availability, utilisation, and capacity of subcontractors and associates across active and upcoming engagements. Match available capacity to staffing demands.

processes.name: Track subcontractor availability and forward commitments
processes.agentic_note: Agents maintain availability calendars and flag conflicts; humans confirm allocations

processes.name: Match subcontractor skills to engagement staffing needs
processes.agentic_note: Agents run skill-matching algorithms; humans make final staffing decisions

processes.name: Monitor subcontractor utilisation rates and cost efficiency
processes.agentic_note: Agents compute utilisation metrics and cost analysis; humans act on insights

domains.name: Finance
domains.subtitle: Funding growth and protecting the bottom line
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the enterprise's ability to plan, allocate, record, and report on financial resources — ensuring fiscal discipline, regulatory compliance, and the financial intelligence needed to support strategic decision-making. It spans from long-range financial planning through day-to-day transaction processing to external reporting and audit readiness.
In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, finance is a domain of high automation potential in transactional processes (accounts payable, reconciliation, reporting) while retaining strong human leadership in judgement-intensive areas such as treasury strategy, tax planning, and stakeholder communication.

capabilities.name: Plan and Analyse Financial Performance
capabilities.description: Develop financial plans, budgets, and forecasts that translate strategic intent into financial targets — and provide ongoing analysis to track performance, explain variances, and inform decisions.

processes.name: Develop annual budgets and long-range financial plans
processes.agentic_note: Humans lead planning; agents compile data and model scenarios

measurements.name: Budget Variance %
measurements.what: Percentage deviation of actuals from approved budget
measurements.why: Indicates accuracy of budget planning and fiscal discipline
measurements.how: (Actual Spend − Budget) / Budget × 100

measurements.name: Budget Cycle Time
measurements.what: Elapsed days from budget kickoff to board approval
measurements.why: Measures efficiency of the budgeting process
measurements.how: Board approval date − budget kickoff date (calendar days)

measurements.name: Long-Range Plan Coverage
measurements.what: Number of years covered by the current financial plan
measurements.why: Ensures strategic planning horizon is adequate for capital decisions
measurements.how: End year of plan − current year

processes.name: Prepare rolling forecasts and outlook updates
processes.agentic_note: Agents generate forecasts from actuals and trends; humans adjust assumptions

measurements.name: Forecast Accuracy
measurements.what: Percentage accuracy of rolling forecast vs actuals
measurements.why: Reliable forecasts enable better resource allocation and decision-making
measurements.how: 1 − |Forecast − Actual| / Actual × 100

measurements.name: Forecast Cycle Time
measurements.what: Days to complete a forecast refresh cycle
measurements.why: Faster cycles enable more responsive planning
measurements.how: Forecast completion date − data cut-off date (business days)

processes.name: Analyse financial performance and explain variances
processes.agentic_note: Agents compute variances and surface drivers; humans interpret and narrate

measurements.name: Variance Report Timeliness
measurements.what: Days after period close that variance analysis is delivered
measurements.why: Timely analysis enables faster corrective action
measurements.how: Variance report delivery date − period close date (business days)

measurements.name: Variance Explanation Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of material variances with documented root-cause explanations
measurements.why: Ensures management understands drivers behind financial performance
measurements.how: Variances with root-cause / Total material variances × 100

processes.name: Model financial scenarios and investment cases
processes.agentic_note: Agents build models; humans define assumptions and evaluate outcomes

measurements.name: Business Case Approval Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of submitted investment cases that receive approval
measurements.why: Indicates quality of case preparation and strategic alignment
measurements.how: Approved cases / Submitted cases × 100

measurements.name: Post-Investment Return Accuracy
measurements.what: Deviation of actual ROI from projected ROI at time of approval
measurements.why: Validates the quality of financial modelling assumptions
measurements.how: |Actual ROI − Projected ROI| / Projected ROI × 100

processes.name: Provide financial decision support to business leaders
processes.agentic_note: Humans advise; agents prepare briefings and ad hoc analysis

measurements.name: Decision Support Request Turnaround
measurements.what: Average hours to respond to ad hoc financial analysis requests
measurements.why: Faster response enables timelier business decisions
measurements.how: Mean elapsed hours from request to delivery

measurements.name: Stakeholder Satisfaction Score
measurements.what: Business leader satisfaction with financial decision support
measurements.why: Measures perceived value and quality of finance business partnering
measurements.how: Average score from quarterly stakeholder survey (1–5 scale)

processes.name: Monitor financial KPIs and early warning indicators
processes.agentic_note: Agent-executed; continuous dashboard monitoring with threshold alerts

measurements.name: Alert Response Time
measurements.what: Average time between KPI threshold breach and management acknowledgement
measurements.why: Fast response to financial alerts prevents escalation of issues
measurements.how: Mean elapsed hours from alert trigger to acknowledgement

measurements.name: False Alert Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of triggered alerts that are false positives
measurements.why: High false alert rates cause alert fatigue and missed genuine issues
measurements.how: False positive alerts / Total alerts triggered × 100

capabilities.name: Manage Accounting and the General Ledger
capabilities.description: Maintain the enterprise's books of record — processing journal entries, managing the chart of accounts, performing reconciliations, and ensuring the general ledger accurately reflects all financial activity.

processes.name: Maintain and govern the chart of accounts
processes.agentic_note: Humans own account structure; agents flag inconsistencies and suggest harmonisation

measurements.name: Chart of Accounts Integrity Score
measurements.what: Percentage of accounts that conform to naming and coding standards
measurements.why: Clean chart of accounts ensures consistent reporting and reduces mispostings
measurements.how: Compliant accounts / Total active accounts × 100

measurements.name: Dormant Account Ratio
measurements.what: Percentage of accounts with no activity in the last 12 months
measurements.why: Excess dormant accounts add complexity and increase error risk
measurements.how: Accounts with zero transactions in 12 months / Total accounts × 100

processes.name: Process journal entries and adjustments
processes.agentic_note: Agents post routine entries; humans review and approve non-standard journals

measurements.name: Journal Entry Error Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of journal entries requiring correction after posting
measurements.why: Errors erode data quality and increase rework during close
measurements.how: Corrected entries / Total entries posted × 100

measurements.name: Automated Journal Entry Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of journal entries posted without manual intervention
measurements.why: Higher automation reduces cost and improves consistency
measurements.how: Auto-posted entries / Total entries × 100

processes.name: Perform account reconciliations
processes.agentic_note: Agent-executed; automated matching with human review of exceptions

measurements.name: Reconciliation Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of accounts reconciled by the target deadline
measurements.why: Incomplete reconciliations delay close and increase risk of misstatement
measurements.how: Accounts reconciled on time / Total accounts requiring reconciliation × 100

measurements.name: Unreconciled Items Age
measurements.what: Average age of open reconciling items in days
measurements.why: Aged unreconciled items indicate systemic issues and increase audit risk
measurements.how: Sum of days outstanding for all open items / Number of open items

measurements.name: Auto-Match Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of reconciling items matched automatically without manual review
measurements.why: Higher auto-match rates reduce reconciliation effort and speed up close
measurements.how: Auto-matched items / Total items to reconcile × 100

processes.name: Manage intercompany transactions and eliminations
processes.agentic_note: Agents process and reconcile; humans resolve disputes and policy questions

measurements.name: Intercompany Balance Match Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of intercompany balances that agree between counterparties
measurements.why: Mismatches delay consolidation and signal process breakdowns
measurements.how: Matched intercompany balances / Total intercompany balances × 100

measurements.name: Intercompany Dispute Resolution Time
measurements.what: Average days to resolve intercompany balance disputes
measurements.why: Prolonged disputes delay close and consume finance team capacity
measurements.how: Mean calendar days from dispute raised to resolution

processes.name: Execute period-end close processes
processes.agentic_note: Agents orchestrate close checklists and track completion; humans manage judgements

measurements.name: Close Cycle Time
measurements.what: Business days from period end to books closed
measurements.why: Faster close provides earlier access to financial data for decisions
measurements.how: Books closed date − period end date (business days)

measurements.name: Close Task Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of close checklist tasks completed on schedule
measurements.why: On-time task completion prevents close delays and bottlenecks
measurements.how: Tasks completed on time / Total close tasks × 100

measurements.name: Post-Close Adjustment Count
measurements.what: Number of material adjustments made after books are closed
measurements.why: Post-close adjustments indicate close quality issues
measurements.how: Count of material journal entries posted after close date

processes.name: Manage fixed asset accounting and depreciation
processes.agentic_note: Agents calculate depreciation and track assets; humans approve disposals

measurements.name: Asset Register Accuracy
measurements.what: Percentage of fixed assets verified as physically present and correctly recorded
measurements.why: Inaccurate registers lead to misstatement and audit findings
measurements.how: Verified assets / Total registered assets × 100

measurements.name: Depreciation Processing Timeliness
measurements.what: Whether depreciation is run and posted by the close deadline
measurements.why: Late depreciation delays close and affects period accuracy
measurements.how: Binary: posted by deadline (Yes/No), tracked as % of periods on time

capabilities.name: Process Accounts Payable and Receivable
capabilities.description: Manage the enterprise's payment obligations to suppliers and the collection of amounts owed by customers — ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and optimal cash flow management.

processes.name: Receive, validate, and code supplier invoices
processes.agentic_note: Agent-executed; automated invoice capture, matching, and coding

measurements.name: Invoice Processing Cycle Time
measurements.what: Average days from invoice receipt to approval-ready status
measurements.why: Faster processing enables early payment discounts and better supplier relations
measurements.how: Mean calendar days from receipt timestamp to approved status

measurements.name: Straight-Through Processing Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of invoices processed without manual intervention
measurements.why: Higher STP reduces cost per invoice and processing time
measurements.how: Auto-processed invoices / Total invoices received × 100

measurements.name: Invoice Exception Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of invoices flagged for exceptions (mismatches, missing data)
measurements.why: High exception rates indicate upstream process issues with POs or receipts
measurements.how: Exception invoices / Total invoices × 100

processes.name: Manage payment runs and disbursements
processes.agentic_note: Agents schedule and execute payments; humans approve above thresholds

measurements.name: On-Time Payment Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of supplier payments made by the due date
measurements.why: Timely payments protect supplier relationships and avoid late fees
measurements.how: Payments made on or before due date / Total payments × 100

measurements.name: Early Payment Discount Capture Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of available early payment discounts captured
measurements.why: Capturing discounts directly improves bottom line
measurements.how: Discounts captured / Total discounts available × 100

processes.name: Manage supplier queries and dispute resolution
processes.agentic_note: Agents track disputes and compile evidence; humans negotiate resolutions

measurements.name: Supplier Dispute Resolution Time
measurements.what: Average days to resolve supplier invoice disputes
measurements.why: Unresolved disputes damage relationships and block payments
measurements.how: Mean calendar days from dispute raised to resolution

measurements.name: Open Dispute Value
measurements.what: Total monetary value of unresolved supplier disputes
measurements.why: High open dispute value indicates process breakdown and cash flow risk
measurements.how: Sum of disputed invoice amounts with status = open

processes.name: Generate and distribute customer invoices
processes.agentic_note: Agent-executed; automated billing based on delivery and contract data

measurements.name: Invoice Accuracy Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of customer invoices issued without errors
measurements.why: Invoice errors delay payment and damage customer relationships
measurements.how: Error-free invoices / Total invoices issued × 100

measurements.name: Billing Timeliness
measurements.what: Percentage of invoices issued within the contractual billing window
measurements.why: Late billing delays cash collection and distorts revenue recognition
measurements.how: Invoices issued on time / Total invoices due × 100

processes.name: Monitor receivables ageing and pursue collections
processes.agentic_note: Agents send reminders and escalate; humans handle sensitive or large accounts

measurements.name: Days Sales Outstanding
measurements.what: Average number of days to collect payment after invoicing
measurements.why: Lower DSO improves cash flow and reduces working capital requirements
measurements.how: (Accounts Receivable / Net Credit Sales) × Number of Days

measurements.name: Collection Effectiveness Index
measurements.what: Effectiveness of collections effort as a percentage
measurements.why: Measures how well the collections process converts receivables to cash
measurements.how: (Beginning Receivables + Credit Sales − Ending Total Receivables) / (Beginning Receivables + Credit Sales − Ending Current Receivables) × 100

measurements.name: Overdue Receivables Ratio
measurements.what: Percentage of total receivables that are past due
measurements.why: High overdue ratio signals credit risk and collection inefficiency
measurements.how: Past-due receivables / Total receivables × 100

processes.name: Process customer payments and apply cash receipts
processes.agentic_note: Agent-executed; automated matching and application with exception routing

measurements.name: Cash Application Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of incoming payments auto-applied to correct invoices
measurements.why: High auto-application reduces manual effort and speeds cash posting
measurements.how: Auto-applied payments / Total payments received × 100

measurements.name: Unapplied Cash Age
measurements.what: Average age in days of unapplied cash receipts
measurements.why: Aged unapplied cash distorts receivables reporting and frustrates customers
measurements.how: Sum of days outstanding for unapplied items / Number of unapplied items

processes.name: Manage credit exposure and bad debt provisions
processes.agentic_note: Humans set credit policy; agents monitor exposure and flag breaches

measurements.name: Bad Debt Write-Off Rate
measurements.what: Bad debt written off as a percentage of total credit sales
measurements.why: Measures effectiveness of credit management and provisioning
measurements.how: Bad debt write-offs / Total credit sales × 100

measurements.name: Credit Limit Breach Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of customers exceeding their approved credit limit
measurements.why: Frequent breaches indicate weak credit controls and elevated risk
measurements.how: Customers over limit / Total credit customers × 100

capabilities.name: Manage Treasury and Cash Operations
capabilities.description: Oversee the enterprise's cash position, liquidity, banking relationships, and financial risk exposures — ensuring sufficient funding is available and financial risks are managed within appetite.

processes.name: Forecast cash flows and manage liquidity positions
processes.agentic_note: Agents generate daily/weekly cash forecasts; humans review and adjust

measurements.name: Cash Forecast Accuracy
measurements.what: Accuracy of short-term cash flow forecasts vs actuals
measurements.why: Accurate cash forecasts prevent liquidity crises and reduce idle cash
measurements.how: 1 − |Forecast Cash Position − Actual Cash Position| / Actual Cash Position × 100

measurements.name: Liquidity Coverage Ratio
measurements.what: Ratio of liquid assets to short-term obligations
measurements.why: Ensures the enterprise can meet near-term payment obligations
measurements.how: High-quality liquid assets / Net cash outflows over 30 days

processes.name: Manage banking relationships and account structures
processes.agentic_note: Humans own bank relationships; agents track fees and service levels

measurements.name: Bank Fee Efficiency
measurements.what: Total bank fees as a percentage of transaction volume
measurements.why: Monitoring fee efficiency ensures competitive banking costs
measurements.how: Total bank fees / Total transaction value × 100

measurements.name: Bank Account Rationalisation Score
measurements.what: Number of active bank accounts relative to operating entities
measurements.why: Excess accounts increase cost, complexity, and fraud risk
measurements.how: Active bank accounts / Number of operating entities

processes.name: Execute and manage investment and borrowing activities
processes.agentic_note: Human-led; agents provide market data and scenario analysis

measurements.name: Weighted Average Cost of Debt
measurements.what: Blended interest rate across all borrowing facilities
measurements.why: Lower cost of debt directly improves profitability
measurements.how: Sum of (each facility's interest × its balance) / Total borrowings

measurements.name: Investment Portfolio Yield
measurements.what: Annualised return on short-term investment portfolio
measurements.why: Maximising yield on surplus cash improves financial performance
measurements.how: Investment income / Average portfolio balance × 100 (annualised)

processes.name: Manage foreign exchange and interest rate exposures
processes.agentic_note: Agents monitor exposures and model hedging; humans approve strategies

measurements.name: FX Hedge Effectiveness
measurements.what: Percentage of FX exposure covered by effective hedges
measurements.why: Effective hedging reduces earnings volatility from currency movements
measurements.how: Hedged FX exposure / Total FX exposure × 100

measurements.name: Realised FX Gain/Loss Ratio
measurements.what: Net FX gains or losses as a percentage of hedged notional
measurements.why: Tracks financial impact of hedging strategy effectiveness
measurements.how: Net realised FX gain or loss / Total hedged notional × 100

processes.name: Perform daily cash positioning and fund transfers
processes.agentic_note: Agents execute routine transfers; humans approve large or unusual movements

measurements.name: Idle Cash Ratio
measurements.what: Percentage of total cash sitting uninvested in non-interest-bearing accounts
measurements.why: Idle cash represents missed investment income
measurements.how: Non-interest-bearing balances / Total cash balances × 100

measurements.name: Fund Transfer Error Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of fund transfers requiring correction or reversal
measurements.why: Transfer errors create reconciliation issues and operational risk
measurements.how: Corrected or reversed transfers / Total transfers × 100

capabilities.name: Plan and Manage Tax Compliance
capabilities.description: Ensure the enterprise meets all tax obligations accurately and on time — while proactively planning to optimise the tax position within legal and ethical boundaries.

processes.name: Develop and maintain the enterprise tax strategy
processes.agentic_note: Human-led; requires professional judgement and regulatory interpretation

measurements.name: Effective Tax Rate
measurements.what: Actual tax paid as a percentage of pre-tax income
measurements.why: Measures effectiveness of tax planning within legal boundaries
measurements.how: Total tax expense / Pre-tax income × 100

measurements.name: Tax Strategy Review Currency
measurements.what: Months since the tax strategy was last reviewed and updated
measurements.why: Stale strategies may not reflect current legislation or business structure
measurements.how: Current date − last strategy review date (months)

processes.name: Calculate and prepare direct and indirect tax returns
processes.agentic_note: Agents compute tax positions and draft returns; humans review and submit

measurements.name: Tax Filing Timeliness
measurements.what: Percentage of tax returns filed by the statutory deadline
measurements.why: Late filings attract penalties and regulatory scrutiny
measurements.how: Returns filed on time / Total returns due × 100

measurements.name: Tax Return Accuracy
measurements.what: Percentage of tax returns filed without subsequent amendment
measurements.why: Amendments indicate calculation errors and increase audit risk
measurements.how: Returns without amendment / Total returns filed × 100

processes.name: Manage transfer pricing documentation and compliance
processes.agentic_note: Agents maintain documentation; humans make policy decisions

measurements.name: Transfer Pricing Documentation Completeness
measurements.what: Percentage of intercompany transactions with current TP documentation
measurements.why: Incomplete documentation creates significant tax audit exposure
measurements.how: Documented transactions / Total intercompany transactions × 100

measurements.name: Arm's Length Compliance Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of transfer prices within the arm's length range
measurements.why: Non-compliant pricing risks penalties and double taxation
measurements.how: Compliant transactions / Total tested transactions × 100

processes.name: Monitor tax legislation changes and assess impact
processes.agentic_note: Agents scan regulatory changes; humans assess strategic implications

measurements.name: Regulatory Change Response Time
measurements.what: Average days from legislation publication to impact assessment completion
measurements.why: Slow response to tax changes risks non-compliance or missed planning opportunities
measurements.how: Mean calendar days from publication to completed assessment

measurements.name: Legislation Monitoring Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of relevant jurisdictions actively monitored for tax changes
measurements.why: Gaps in monitoring create compliance blind spots
measurements.how: Monitored jurisdictions / Total jurisdictions with tax obligations × 100

processes.name: Manage tax audits and authority inquiries

capabilities.name: Maintain Financial Controls and Support Audit
capabilities.description: Design, operate, and test the internal control environment over financial reporting and transactions — ensuring the enterprise can demonstrate effective governance and withstand internal and external audit scrutiny.

processes.name: Design and document financial controls and control frameworks

measurements.name: Control Documentation Currency

measurements.name: Control Coverage Ratio

processes.name: Test control effectiveness and remediate deficiencies

measurements.name: Control Deficiency Rate

measurements.name: Deficiency Remediation Timeliness

processes.name: Support internal audit engagement and follow-up

measurements.name: Audit Finding Closure Rate

measurements.name: Access Review Completion Rate
