domains.name: People & Talent
domains.subtitle: Attracting and orchestrating the blended workforce
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the enterprise's ability to attract, develop, engage, and retain the human talent it needs — while simultaneously provisioning, calibrating, and managing the agent workforce that operates alongside them. It spans the full employee lifecycle from workforce planning through separation, and extends into the uniquely ZORBA territory of blended workforce orchestration.
In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, this domain is where the human and agent workforces are designed, composed, and harmonised — ensuring that every role, whether filled by a person or an agent, is deliberately planned, appropriately skilled, and continuously optimised.

capabilities.name: Plan and Analyse the Workforce
capabilities.description: Forecast workforce demand, assess current capacity, identify skills gaps, and build strategic workforce plans that inform talent acquisition, development, and deployment decisions.

processes.name: Forecast workforce demand by role, skill, and location
processes.agentic_note: Agents model demand using business plans and attrition data; humans validate assumptions

measurements.name: Forecast Accuracy Rate
measurements.what: Accuracy of workforce demand forecasts vs actual headcount needs
measurements.why: Ensures workforce plans reflect real business demand
measurements.how: (Forecasted positions - Actual positions needed) / Forecasted positions x 100

measurements.name: Forecast Horizon Coverage
measurements.what: Number of quarters ahead with completed workforce forecasts
measurements.why: Longer horizons enable proactive talent pipeline building
measurements.how: Count of future quarters with approved forecasts

processes.name: Assess current workforce capacity and skills inventory
processes.agentic_note: Agents aggregate skills data from multiple systems; humans verify accuracy

measurements.name: Skills Inventory Completeness
measurements.what: Percentage of employees with up-to-date skills profiles
measurements.why: Accurate skills data enables effective workforce deployment
measurements.how: Employees with current skills profile / Total employees x 100

measurements.name: Skills Data Freshness
measurements.what: Average age of employee skills assessment data in days
measurements.why: Stale data leads to poor workforce planning decisions
measurements.how: Average days since last skills update across all employees

processes.name: Identify critical skills gaps and surplus areas
processes.agentic_note: Agents surface gaps through analytics; humans interpret strategic implications

measurements.name: Critical Skills Gap Ratio
measurements.what: Percentage of critical roles with identified skills gaps
measurements.why: Highlights strategic vulnerability in workforce capability
measurements.how: Critical roles with gaps / Total critical roles x 100

measurements.name: Gap Closure Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of identified skills gaps addressed within target timeframe
measurements.why: Measures effectiveness of gap remediation efforts
measurements.how: Gaps closed on time / Total identified gaps x 100

processes.name: Develop strategic workforce plans and scenarios
processes.agentic_note: Humans design workforce strategy; agents model cost and feasibility scenarios

measurements.name: Workforce Plan Adoption Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of business units with approved strategic workforce plans
measurements.why: Ensures workforce planning is enterprise-wide
measurements.how: Units with approved plans / Total business units x 100

measurements.name: Scenario Modelling Coverage
measurements.what: Number of workforce scenarios modelled per planning cycle
measurements.why: Multiple scenarios improve resilience of workforce strategy
measurements.how: Count of distinct scenarios modelled and reviewed

processes.name: Monitor workforce metrics and trigger plan adjustments
processes.agentic_note: Agent-executed; continuous monitoring with automated alerting on threshold breaches

measurements.name: Workforce Metric Alert Response Time
measurements.what: Average time to respond to workforce metric threshold breaches
measurements.why: Faster response prevents workforce issues from escalating
measurements.how: Average hours from alert trigger to documented response action

measurements.name: Plan Adjustment Frequency
measurements.what: Number of proactive workforce plan adjustments triggered by monitoring
measurements.why: Indicates monitoring system is driving actionable insights
measurements.how: Count of plan adjustments initiated from monitoring alerts per quarter

capabilities.name: Acquire Talent and Onboard New Starters
capabilities.description: Attract, select, and hire the right people, then integrate them effectively into the organisation through structured onboarding that accelerates time-to-productivity.

processes.name: Define role requirements and craft position profiles

measurements.name: Position Profile Completion Rate

measurements.how: Roles with complete profiles / Total open roles x 100

measurements.name: Profile Creation Cycle Time
measurements.what: Average days to create and approve a position profile

measurements.how: Average business days from request to approved profile

processes.name: Source and attract candidates through multiple channels
processes.agentic_note: Agents manage job postings, screen CVs, and engage passive talent; humans curate shortlists

measurements.name: Qualified Applicants per Role
measurements.what: Average number of qualified candidates per open position
measurements.why: Indicates effectiveness of sourcing strategy
measurements.how: Total qualified applicants / Total open positions

measurements.name: Source Channel Effectiveness
measurements.what: Hire rate from top-performing sourcing channel

processes.name: Assess and select candidates through structured evaluation

measurements.name: Interview-to-Offer Ratio
measurements.what: Number of interviews conducted per offer extended

measurements.how: Total interviews / Total offers extended

measurements.name: Assessment Predictive Validity

measurements.name: Pre-Employment Process Cycle Time

measurements.name: New Hire Satisfaction Score

measurements.how: Average score on post-onboarding survey (1-10 scale)

processes.name: Evaluate onboarding effectiveness and time-to-productivity

measurements.name: Time to Productivity

measurements.how: Average days from start date to meeting role performance benchmarks

measurements.name: 90-Day Retention Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of new hires still employed after 90 days
measurements.why: Early attrition indicates onboarding or selection failures
measurements.how: New hires retained at 90 days / Total new hires x 100

measurements.name: Mentee Satisfaction Score

processes.name: Measure learning impact and return on investment

measurements.name: Learning ROI

measurements.name: Skill Application Rate

measurements.what: Adjusted pay gap between demographic groups for comparable roles

measurements.name: Remediation Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of pay equity issues remediated within target timeframe

capabilities.description: Shape the end-to-end employee experience — from cultural environment to wellbeing — and actively monitor and improve workforce engagement to drive retention and performance.

measurements.name: Employee Experience Score

measurements.name: Experience Friction Points Resolved

measurements.name: Survey Response Rate

measurements.name: Wellbeing Programme Utilisation

measurements.name: Absenteeism Rate

processes.name: Foster workplace culture and inclusion

measurements.name: Inclusion Index Score

measurements.name: Diversity Representation Rate

processes.name: Manage internal employee communication channels

measurements.name: Internal Comms Reach Rate

measurements.name: Communication Satisfaction Score

capabilities.name: Manage the Blended Workforce
capabilities.description: ZORBA-specific capability. Provision, configure, monitor, and govern the agent workforce alongside the human workforce — ensuring agents are treated as managed participants in the operating model with defined roles, performance expectations, trust boundaries, and lifecycle management.

processes.name: Provision and configure new agents into the workforce

processes.name: Define agent roles, responsibilities, and trust boundaries
processes.agentic_note: Humans define trust levels and guardrails; agents maintain the role registry

measurements.name: Agent Role Definition Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of active agents with documented roles and trust boundaries
measurements.why: Undefined boundaries create governance and safety risks
measurements.how: Agents with complete role definitions / Total active agents x 100

measurements.name: Trust Boundary Violation Rate
measurements.what: Number of agent actions outside defined trust boundaries per 1000 actions
measurements.why: Violations indicate misconfiguration or policy gaps
measurements.how: Boundary violations / Total agent actions x 1000

processes.name: Monitor agent performance and behavioural compliance
processes.agentic_note: Agents monitor agent fleet metrics and flag anomalies; humans investigate and intervene

measurements.name: Agent Performance SLA Compliance
measurements.what: Percentage of agents meeting defined performance SLAs
measurements.why: Underperforming agents reduce blended workforce effectiveness
measurements.how: Agents meeting SLA / Total active agents x 100

measurements.name: Agent Anomaly Detection Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of agent anomalies detected before business impact
measurements.why: Early detection prevents downstream errors and compliance breaches
measurements.how: Anomalies detected proactively / Total anomalies x 100

processes.name: Calibrate agent trust levels and autonomy thresholds
processes.agentic_note: Humans set trust policy; agents track performance and recommend recalibration

measurements.name: Trust Level Calibration Frequency
measurements.what: Average number of trust level reviews per agent per year
measurements.why: Regular calibration ensures trust levels reflect current performance
measurements.how: Total trust reviews / Total active agents

measurements.name: Trust Escalation Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of agents with trust levels increased based on performance
measurements.why: Progression indicates agents are maturing and earning autonomy
measurements.how: Agents with increased trust / Total agents reviewed x 100

processes.name: Manage the agent lifecycle — updates, retraining, and retirement
processes.agentic_note: Agents execute lifecycle workflows; humans approve major transitions and decommissioning

measurements.name: Agent Update Compliance Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of agents on current approved versions
measurements.why: Outdated agents may have security or performance issues
measurements.how: Agents on current version / Total active agents x 100

measurements.name: Agent Retirement Timeliness
measurements.what: Percentage of deprecated agents retired within target timeframe
measurements.why: Lingering deprecated agents consume resources and create risk
measurements.how: Agents retired on time / Total flagged for retirement x 100

processes.name: Coordinate human-agent teaming and workload distribution
processes.agentic_note: Agents optimise task routing; humans oversee balance and intervene on conflicts

measurements.name: Human-Agent Task Routing Accuracy
measurements.what: Percentage of tasks routed to the optimal executor
measurements.why: Accurate routing maximises blended workforce productivity
measurements.how: Correctly routed tasks / Total routed tasks x 100

measurements.name: Workload Balance Index
measurements.what: Variance in workload utilisation across human-agent teams
measurements.why: Balanced workloads prevent burnout and underutilisation
measurements.how: Standard deviation of utilisation rates across team members

processes.name: Report on blended workforce composition and effectiveness
processes.agentic_note: Agents generate dashboards and trend reports; humans interpret and present to leadership

measurements.name: Blended Workforce Report Timeliness
measurements.what: Percentage of workforce composition reports delivered on schedule
measurements.why: Timely reporting enables proactive workforce management
measurements.how: On-time reports / Total scheduled reports x 100

measurements.name: Agent-to-Human Ratio Trend
measurements.what: Ratio of agent FTE-equivalents to human FTEs over time
measurements.why: Tracks evolution of the blended workforce composition
measurements.how: Agent FTE-equivalents / Human FTEs

domains.name: Technology & Data
domains.subtitle: Powering the enterprise with platforms and insight
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the enterprise's ability to design, build, operate, and evolve its technology landscape — from core infrastructure and applications through to data platforms, cybersecurity, and end-user technology services. It ensures technology investments align with strategic intent and that data is treated as a first-class enterprise asset.
In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, this domain carries a unique additional responsibility: managing the agent platform itself. The technology function not only deploys agents — it operates the infrastructure, tooling, and guardrails that make the blended workforce possible.

capabilities.name: Shape Technology Strategy and Architecture
capabilities.description: Define the technology vision, principles, and enterprise architecture that guide investment decisions, platform choices, and the evolution of the technology estate.

processes.name: Define the enterprise technology vision and principles
processes.agentic_note: Human-led; technology strategy requires human judgement on business priorities

measurements.name: Technology Strategy Alignment Score
measurements.what: Degree of alignment between tech investments and business strategy
measurements.why: Misaligned tech spending wastes resources and delays strategic goals
measurements.how: Percentage of tech projects mapped to strategic objectives

measurements.name: Technology Vision Refresh Cadence
measurements.what: Months since last technology vision review and update
measurements.why: Stale vision leads to outdated investment decisions
measurements.how: Months elapsed since last board-approved vision update

processes.name: Develop and maintain enterprise architecture standards
processes.agentic_note: Agents maintain architecture artefacts; humans set standards and make trade-offs

measurements.name: Architecture Standards Adoption Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of new projects conforming to enterprise architecture standards
measurements.why: Non-conformance increases integration cost and technical debt
measurements.how: Conforming projects / Total new projects x 100

measurements.name: Architecture Artefact Currency
measurements.what: Percentage of architecture documents updated within review cycle
measurements.why: Outdated artefacts lead to inconsistent design decisions
measurements.how: Current artefacts / Total artefacts x 100

processes.name: Assess emerging technologies and evaluate adoption readiness
processes.agentic_note: Agents scan, evaluate, and benchmark; humans decide strategic relevance

measurements.name: Emerging Technology Assessments Completed
measurements.what: Number of formal technology evaluations completed per quarter
measurements.why: Continuous scanning prevents strategic technology blind spots
measurements.how: Count of completed technology assessment reports

measurements.name: Technology Adoption Lead Time
measurements.what: Average months from technology evaluation to production pilot
measurements.why: Faster adoption captures competitive advantage sooner
measurements.how: Average months from assessment completion to pilot launch

processes.name: Manage the technology investment portfolio and roadmap
processes.agentic_note: Humans prioritise investments; agents model costs and dependencies

measurements.name: IT Investment Portfolio ROI
measurements.what: Return on investment across the technology portfolio
measurements.why: Ensures technology investments deliver business value
measurements.how: (Total benefits realised - Total IT investment) / Total IT investment x 100

measurements.name: Roadmap Delivery Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of roadmap milestones delivered on schedule
measurements.why: Slippage delays business capabilities and erodes stakeholder confidence
measurements.how: On-time milestones / Total milestones x 100

processes.name: Govern architecture conformance and technical debt
processes.agentic_note: Agents detect drift and debt; humans approve remediation priorities

measurements.name: Architecture Conformance Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of systems conforming to architecture standards
measurements.why: Non-conformance accumulates technical debt and integration risk
measurements.how: Conforming systems / Total systems x 100

measurements.name: Technical Debt Ratio
measurements.what: Estimated technical debt as percentage of total technology asset value
measurements.why: High debt increases maintenance cost and slows delivery
measurements.how: Estimated remediation cost / Total technology asset value x 100

capabilities.name: Build and Manage Applications
capabilities.description: Design, develop, test, deploy, and maintain the enterprise's application portfolio — including custom-built, configured, and integrated software solutions.

processes.name: Gather and prioritise application requirements
processes.agentic_note: Agents collect and structure requirements; humans validate and prioritise

measurements.name: Requirements Clarity Score
measurements.what: Percentage of requirements accepted without rework after review
measurements.why: Clear requirements reduce development waste and rework
measurements.how: Requirements accepted first time / Total requirements submitted x 100

measurements.name: Backlog Grooming Cycle Time
measurements.what: Average days from requirement submission to prioritised backlog entry
measurements.why: Faster grooming accelerates delivery pipeline flow
measurements.how: Average days from submission to backlog placement

processes.name: Design and architect application solutions
processes.agentic_note: Humans design; agents generate reference architectures and patterns

measurements.name: Design Review Pass Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of solution designs approved at first review
measurements.why: Rework in design is cheaper than rework in code
measurements.how: Designs approved first time / Total designs reviewed x 100

measurements.name: Pattern Reuse Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of new designs leveraging existing architecture patterns
measurements.why: Reuse accelerates delivery and reduces risk
measurements.how: Designs using existing patterns / Total new designs x 100

processes.name: Develop and configure application code
processes.agentic_note: Agents generate and refactor code; humans review, approve, and guide

measurements.name: Code Quality Score
measurements.what: Composite code quality metric from static analysis
measurements.why: Higher quality code reduces bugs and maintenance burden
measurements.how: Weighted score from linting, complexity, and duplication metrics (0-100)

measurements.name: Developer Velocity
measurements.what: Story points or features delivered per sprint per developer
measurements.why: Tracks development team productivity over time
measurements.how: Total story points completed / Number of developers per sprint

processes.name: Test and validate application quality
processes.agentic_note: Agents execute automated test suites; humans define test strategies and review edge cases

measurements.name: Defect Escape Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of defects found in production vs total defects
measurements.why: Lower escape rate means testing catches more issues before release
measurements.how: Production defects / Total defects found x 100

measurements.name: Automated Test Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of code covered by automated tests
measurements.why: Higher coverage reduces regression risk and enables faster releases
measurements.how: Lines covered by automated tests / Total lines of code x 100

processes.name: Deploy and release applications to production
processes.agentic_note: Agents orchestrate CI/CD pipelines; humans approve production releases

measurements.name: Deployment Frequency
measurements.what: Number of production deployments per month
measurements.why: Higher frequency indicates mature CI/CD and faster value delivery
measurements.how: Count of successful production deployments per month

measurements.name: Deployment Failure Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of deployments requiring rollback or hotfix
measurements.why: Failed deployments cause outages and erode confidence
measurements.how: Failed deployments / Total deployments x 100

processes.name: Manage the application portfolio lifecycle
processes.agentic_note: Agents track usage, costs, and health; humans decide rationalisation actions

measurements.name: Application Portfolio Health Score
measurements.what: Percentage of applications rated as healthy in portfolio assessment
measurements.why: Unhealthy apps increase risk and maintenance cost
measurements.how: Healthy-rated applications / Total applications x 100

measurements.name: Application Rationalisation Progress
measurements.what: Percentage of targeted applications retired or consolidated
measurements.why: Reducing portfolio complexity lowers cost and risk
measurements.how: Applications retired or consolidated / Total targeted x 100

capabilities.name: Operate Infrastructure and Cloud Platforms
capabilities.description: Provision, manage, and optimise the enterprise's computing infrastructure — including on-premises, cloud, hybrid, and edge environments.

processes.name: Provision and configure infrastructure resources
processes.agentic_note: Agents execute infrastructure-as-code; humans define policies and approve changes

measurements.name: Infrastructure Provisioning Time
measurements.what: Average time to provision new infrastructure resources
measurements.why: Faster provisioning accelerates project delivery
measurements.how: Average hours from request to available resource

measurements.name: Infrastructure-as-Code Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of infrastructure managed via code templates
measurements.why: IaC ensures consistency, auditability, and repeatability
measurements.how: IaC-managed resources / Total infrastructure resources x 100

processes.name: Monitor infrastructure health and performance
processes.agentic_note: Agent-autonomous; continuous monitoring with human-defined alert thresholds

measurements.name: Infrastructure Availability
measurements.what: Percentage uptime of critical infrastructure services
measurements.why: Downtime directly impacts business operations and revenue
measurements.how: Uptime minutes / Total minutes in period x 100

measurements.name: Mean Time to Detect
measurements.what: Average time to detect infrastructure issues
measurements.why: Faster detection enables faster resolution and reduces impact
measurements.how: Average minutes from issue occurrence to alert

processes.name: Manage capacity planning and resource optimisation
processes.agentic_note: Agents forecast and recommend; humans approve scaling decisions

measurements.name: Capacity Utilisation Rate
measurements.what: Average utilisation of provisioned compute and storage resources
measurements.why: Low utilisation wastes spend; high utilisation risks performance
measurements.how: Average resource utilisation across compute and storage (%)

measurements.name: Capacity Forecast Accuracy
measurements.what: Accuracy of capacity forecasts vs actual demand
measurements.why: Accurate forecasts prevent over-provisioning and outages
measurements.how: 1 - |Forecast - Actual| / Actual x 100

processes.name: Operate cloud platform services and governance
processes.agentic_note: Agents enforce guardrails and track spend; humans set cloud strategy

measurements.name: Cloud Spend Variance
measurements.what: Deviation of actual cloud spend from budget
measurements.why: Uncontrolled cloud spend erodes financial discipline
measurements.how: (Actual cloud spend - Budget) / Budget x 100

measurements.name: Cloud Governance Compliance Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of cloud resources compliant with governance policies
measurements.why: Non-compliant resources create security and cost risks
measurements.how: Compliant resources / Total cloud resources x 100

processes.name: Execute infrastructure auto-remediation and self-healing
processes.agentic_note: Agent-autonomous; automated remediation within predefined runbooks

measurements.name: Auto-Remediation Success Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of automated remediations that resolve issues without escalation
measurements.why: Higher success reduces human workload and speeds recovery
measurements.how: Successful auto-remediations / Total auto-remediation attempts x 100

measurements.name: Mean Time to Recover (Automated)
measurements.what: Average time for automated self-healing to restore service
measurements.why: Faster recovery minimises business impact
measurements.how: Average minutes from issue detection to service restoration (automated)

processes.name: Manage network and connectivity services
processes.agentic_note: Agents monitor and configure; humans approve topology changes

measurements.name: Network Availability
measurements.what: Percentage uptime of core network services
measurements.why: Network outages affect all dependent services
measurements.how: Network uptime minutes / Total minutes in period x 100

measurements.name: Network Latency Performance
measurements.what: Average network latency against defined thresholds
measurements.why: High latency degrades application performance and user experience
measurements.how: Average round-trip latency in milliseconds for key routes

capabilities.name: Govern Data and Deliver Analytics
capabilities.description: Manage data as an enterprise asset — ensuring quality, accessibility, governance, and the delivery of analytics and insights that drive decision-making.

processes.name: Define data governance frameworks and ownership
processes.agentic_note: Humans define governance; agents maintain catalogues and lineage

measurements.name: Data Governance Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of critical data domains with assigned data owners
measurements.why: Unowned data lacks accountability for quality and access
measurements.how: Domains with assigned owners / Total critical data domains x 100

measurements.name: Data Catalogue Completeness
measurements.what: Percentage of data assets catalogued with metadata
measurements.why: Uncatalogued data is undiscoverable and ungoverned
measurements.how: Catalogued data assets / Total known data assets x 100

processes.name: Manage data quality, profiling, and remediation
processes.agentic_note: Agents profile and cleanse data; humans set quality thresholds and resolve exceptions

measurements.name: Data Quality Score
measurements.what: Composite data quality score across dimensions
measurements.why: Poor data quality leads to unreliable analytics and decisions
measurements.how: Weighted average of completeness, accuracy, consistency, and timeliness scores (0-100)

measurements.name: Data Quality Issue Resolution Time
measurements.what: Average days to resolve identified data quality issues
measurements.why: Lingering quality issues compound downstream impact
measurements.how: Average days from issue detection to resolution

processes.name: Build and operate data pipelines and integration
processes.agentic_note: Agent-executed; automated pipeline orchestration with human-defined schemas

measurements.name: Pipeline Reliability Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of data pipeline runs completing successfully
measurements.why: Failed pipelines delay analytics and reporting
measurements.how: Successful pipeline runs / Total scheduled runs x 100

measurements.name: Data Freshness SLA Compliance
measurements.what: Percentage of data sets refreshed within defined SLA windows
measurements.why: Stale data leads to outdated decisions
measurements.how: Data sets refreshed within SLA / Total SLA-governed data sets x 100

processes.name: Deliver business intelligence and reporting
processes.agentic_note: Agents generate dashboards and reports; humans interpret and act on insights

measurements.name: Report Delivery Timeliness
measurements.what: Percentage of scheduled reports delivered on time
measurements.why: Late reports delay decision-making
measurements.how: On-time reports / Total scheduled reports x 100

measurements.name: BI User Adoption Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of licensed users actively using BI tools
measurements.why: Low adoption wastes licensing investment
measurements.how: Active users in last 30 days / Total licensed users x 100

processes.name: Develop and manage advanced analytics and models
processes.agentic_note: Agents train and tune models; humans validate methodology and interpret results

measurements.name: Model Accuracy
measurements.what: Average prediction accuracy of production analytics models
measurements.why: Inaccurate models lead to poor automated decisions
measurements.how: Average accuracy metric across production models (model-specific)

measurements.name: Model Drift Detection Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of model drift events detected before impact
measurements.why: Undetected drift degrades model performance silently
measurements.how: Drift events detected proactively / Total drift events x 100

processes.name: Manage master data and reference data
processes.agentic_note: Agents maintain records; humans resolve conflicts and approve golden records

measurements.name: Master Data Match Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of records with confirmed golden record linkage
measurements.why: Unlinked records create duplicates and inconsistencies
measurements.how: Linked records / Total master data records x 100

measurements.name: Reference Data Currency
measurements.what: Percentage of reference data sets updated within review cycle
measurements.why: Stale reference data causes mapping and classification errors
measurements.how: Current reference data sets / Total reference data sets x 100

capabilities.name: Protect Information and Ensure Cyber Resilience
capabilities.description: Defend the enterprise against cyber threats, manage information security risks, and ensure resilience through detection, response, and recovery capabilities.

processes.name: Define cybersecurity strategy and risk posture
processes.agentic_note: Human-led; security strategy requires executive judgement and risk appetite alignment

measurements.name: Security Risk Posture Score
measurements.what: Composite cybersecurity risk score from assessment framework
measurements.why: Provides executive visibility into overall security health
measurements.how: Weighted score from security framework assessment (0-100)

measurements.name: Security Strategy Execution Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of security strategy initiatives on track
measurements.why: Delayed initiatives leave the enterprise exposed to known risks
measurements.how: Initiatives on track / Total planned initiatives x 100

processes.name: Manage identity, access, and authentication controls
processes.agentic_note: Agents enforce policies and detect anomalies; humans approve access exceptions

measurements.name: Access Review Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of access reviews completed on schedule
measurements.why: Incomplete reviews leave stale or excessive access undetected
measurements.how: Completed reviews / Scheduled reviews x 100

measurements.name: Privileged Access Anomaly Rate
measurements.what: Number of anomalous privileged access events per month
measurements.why: Anomalous privileged access is a top indicator of compromise
measurements.how: Count of flagged privileged access anomalies

processes.name: Monitor threats and operate security operations
processes.agentic_note: Agent-autonomous; continuous threat monitoring and initial incident triage

measurements.name: Threat Detection Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of simulated attacks detected by security operations
measurements.why: Measures effectiveness of detection capabilities
measurements.how: Simulated attacks detected / Total simulated attacks x 100

measurements.name: Mean Time to Detect Threats
measurements.what: Average time from threat activity to detection alert
measurements.why: Faster detection limits attacker dwell time and damage
measurements.how: Average minutes from threat activity to SOC alert

processes.name: Respond to and recover from security incidents
processes.agentic_note: Humans lead incident response; agents execute containment playbooks and collect forensics

measurements.name: Incident Response Time
measurements.what: Average time from incident declaration to containment
measurements.why: Faster containment limits damage and data exposure
measurements.how: Average minutes from incident declaration to containment confirmation

measurements.name: Incident Recovery Time
measurements.what: Average time from containment to full service restoration
measurements.why: Faster recovery minimises business disruption
measurements.how: Average hours from containment to service restoration

processes.name: Conduct vulnerability management and penetration testing
processes.agentic_note: Agents scan and prioritise vulnerabilities; humans validate findings and approve remediation

measurements.name: Critical Vulnerability Remediation Time
measurements.what: Average days to remediate critical vulnerabilities
measurements.why: Unpatched critical vulnerabilities are prime attack vectors
measurements.how: Average days from critical vulnerability discovery to remediation

measurements.name: Vulnerability Scan Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of IT assets scanned within the scheduled cycle
measurements.why: Unscanned assets may harbour unknown vulnerabilities
measurements.how: Assets scanned / Total IT assets x 100

processes.name: Manage security awareness and training
processes.agentic_note: Agents deliver training and simulate phishing; humans design curriculum and review results

measurements.name: Security Training Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of employees completing mandatory security training
measurements.why: Untrained employees are the weakest link in security defence
measurements.how: Employees completed / Total employees required x 100

measurements.name: Phishing Simulation Click Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of employees clicking simulated phishing links
measurements.why: Lower click rate indicates better security awareness
measurements.how: Employees who clicked / Total employees tested x 100

capabilities.name: Deliver Technology Operations and End-User Support
capabilities.description: Operate the day-to-day technology services that keep the enterprise running — including service desk, incident management, problem resolution, and end-user computing.

processes.name: Operate the service desk and manage service requests
processes.agentic_note: Agents handle tier-0/1 requests; humans manage complex escalations

measurements.name: Service Desk First-Contact Resolution
measurements.what: Percentage of service requests resolved at first contact
measurements.why: Higher FCR improves user satisfaction and reduces costs
measurements.how: Requests resolved at first contact / Total requests x 100

measurements.name: Service Request Fulfilment Time
measurements.what: Average time to fulfil service requests
measurements.why: Faster fulfilment improves employee productivity
measurements.how: Average business hours from request to fulfilment

processes.name: Manage incidents and restore services
processes.agentic_note: Agents detect, categorise, and initiate response; humans lead major incident resolution

measurements.name: Mean Time to Restore Service
measurements.what: Average time to restore services after incidents
measurements.why: Faster restoration minimises business impact
measurements.how: Average minutes from incident to service restoration

measurements.name: Major Incident Frequency
measurements.what: Number of major incidents per month
measurements.why: Fewer major incidents indicates more stable operations
measurements.how: Count of P1/P2 incidents per month

processes.name: Conduct problem management and root cause analysis
processes.agentic_note: Humans lead analysis; agents correlate data and suggest patterns

measurements.name: Problem Resolution Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of identified problems with confirmed root cause resolution
measurements.why: Unresolved problems lead to recurring incidents
measurements.how: Problems resolved / Total problems identified x 100

measurements.name: Repeat Incident Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of incidents linked to previously identified problems
measurements.why: High repeat rate indicates ineffective problem management
measurements.how: Repeat incidents / Total incidents x 100

processes.name: Manage change and release processes
processes.agentic_note: Agents assess impact and schedule changes; humans approve and review

measurements.name: Change Success Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of changes implemented without causing incidents
measurements.why: Failed changes are a leading cause of outages
measurements.how: Successful changes / Total changes implemented x 100

measurements.name: Emergency Change Ratio
measurements.what: Percentage of changes classified as emergency
measurements.why: High emergency change ratio indicates poor planning
measurements.how: Emergency changes / Total changes x 100

processes.name: Provision and manage end-user devices and workplace technology
processes.agentic_note: Agents automate provisioning and configuration; humans handle exceptions

measurements.name: Device Provisioning Time
measurements.what: Average time to provision and deliver end-user devices
measurements.why: Delays impact new starter productivity
measurements.how: Average business days from request to device delivered

measurements.name: Device Compliance Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of end-user devices compliant with security policies
measurements.why: Non-compliant devices create security vulnerabilities
measurements.how: Compliant devices / Total managed devices x 100

capabilities.name: Manage the Agent Platform
capabilities.description: Operate, evolve, and govern the infrastructure that enables enterprise AI agents — including agent hosting, orchestration, lifecycle management, and the tooling that connects agents to enterprise systems. This is the ZORBA-specific meta-capability: the technology that enables the blended workforce.

processes.name: Operate agent hosting infrastructure and runtime environments
processes.agentic_note: Agents monitor their own platform; humans manage capacity and architecture

measurements.name: Agent Platform Availability
measurements.what: Percentage uptime of the agent hosting platform
measurements.why: Platform downtime disables the entire agent workforce
measurements.how: Platform uptime minutes / Total minutes x 100

measurements.name: Agent Platform Response Latency
measurements.what: Average response latency of the agent runtime environment
measurements.why: High latency degrades agent performance and user experience
measurements.how: Average milliseconds for agent API response (p95)

processes.name: Manage agent lifecycle — provisioning, versioning, and retirement
processes.agentic_note: Humans approve agent deployment; agents manage version control and rollback

measurements.name: Agent Version Currency
measurements.what: Percentage of agents running the latest approved version
measurements.why: Outdated versions miss fixes and improvements
measurements.how: Agents on latest version / Total active agents x 100

measurements.name: Agent Rollback Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of agent version updates requiring rollback
measurements.why: High rollback rate indicates poor release quality
measurements.how: Rollbacks / Total version updates x 100

processes.name: Govern agent access to enterprise systems and data
processes.agentic_note: Human-led; access decisions require accountability and policy alignment

measurements.name: Agent Access Policy Compliance
measurements.what: Percentage of agent system access conforming to approved policies
measurements.why: Non-compliant access creates security and data risk
measurements.how: Compliant access grants / Total agent access grants x 100

measurements.name: Agent Access Review Completion
measurements.what: Percentage of agent access reviews completed on schedule
measurements.why: Stale access grants accumulate excessive permissions
measurements.how: Completed reviews / Scheduled reviews x 100

processes.name: Monitor agent performance, health, and resource consumption
processes.agentic_note: Agent-autonomous; agents monitor agent fleet health and flag anomalies

measurements.name: Agent Fleet Health Score
measurements.what: Percentage of agents in healthy operational state
measurements.why: Unhealthy agents fail silently and degrade outcomes
measurements.how: Healthy agents / Total active agents x 100

measurements.name: Agent Resource Efficiency
measurements.what: Average compute cost per agent transaction
measurements.why: Rising costs per transaction indicate inefficiency
measurements.how: Total agent compute cost / Total agent transactions

processes.name: Manage agent orchestration and inter-agent coordination
processes.agentic_note: Agents execute orchestration; humans define workflow boundaries and escalation rules

measurements.name: Orchestration Success Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of multi-agent workflows completing successfully
measurements.why: Failed orchestrations disrupt automated business processes
measurements.how: Successful workflows / Total orchestrated workflows x 100

measurements.name: Inter-Agent Latency
measurements.what: Average latency for agent-to-agent communication
measurements.why: High latency in coordination slows workflow completion
measurements.how: Average milliseconds for inter-agent message round-trip

processes.name: Maintain the agent tooling ecosystem and integration layer
processes.agentic_note: Agents maintain integrations; humans approve new tool access and capabilities

measurements.name: Agent Tool Integration Availability
measurements.what: Percentage uptime of agent-to-system integrations
measurements.why: Integration outages disable agent capabilities
measurements.how: Integration uptime / Total integration time x 100

measurements.name: New Tool Onboarding Time
measurements.what: Average days to integrate a new tool into the agent ecosystem
measurements.why: Faster onboarding expands agent capabilities sooner
measurements.how: Average days from tool request to production integration

processes.name: Manage agent platform security and isolation
processes.agentic_note: Humans set security policies; agents enforce isolation and detect breaches

measurements.name: Agent Isolation Breach Rate
measurements.what: Number of agent isolation boundary violations detected
measurements.why: Breaches indicate security control failures
measurements.how: Count of isolation violations per month

measurements.name: Agent Security Audit Pass Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of agents passing security compliance audits
measurements.why: Non-compliant agents pose risk to enterprise security
measurements.how: Agents passing audit / Total agents audited x 100

domains.name: Risk, Legal & Compliance
domains.subtitle: Governing trust in a human-agent enterprise
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the enterprise's ability to identify, assess, and mitigate risk, provide legal counsel, ensure regulatory compliance, conduct independent assurance, and uphold ethical standards. It is the domain where organisational trust is built, protected, and demonstrated to regulators, customers, and the public.
In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, this domain bears a critical additional responsibility: governing the behaviour, compliance, and trustworthiness of AI agents — ensuring algorithmic decisions are explainable, auditable, and aligned with regulatory expectations and organisational values.

capabilities.name: Manage Enterprise Risk
capabilities.description: Identify, assess, treat, and monitor risks across the enterprise — maintaining the risk register, operating risk frameworks, and ensuring risks are owned, communicated, and actively managed.

processes.name: Maintain the enterprise risk framework and taxonomy
processes.agentic_note: Humans design the framework; agents maintain documentation and version control

measurements.name: Risk Framework Maturity Score
measurements.what: Maturity level of the enterprise risk framework
measurements.why: Higher maturity indicates more robust risk management
measurements.how: Score from independent framework maturity assessment (1-5 scale)

measurements.name: Risk Taxonomy Coverage
measurements.what: Percentage of business domains covered by the risk taxonomy
measurements.why: Gaps in taxonomy leave risks unidentified
measurements.how: Domains with risk taxonomy mapping / Total domains x 100

processes.name: Identify and register risks across domains and units
processes.agentic_note: Agents scan for emerging risks and prompt registration; humans validate and classify

measurements.name: Risk Register Completeness
measurements.what: Percentage of identified risks with full register entries
measurements.why: Incomplete entries prevent effective risk management
measurements.how: Fully documented risks / Total identified risks x 100

measurements.name: Emerging Risk Identification Rate
measurements.what: Number of new risks identified and registered per quarter
measurements.why: Active identification prevents surprise risk events
measurements.how: Count of newly registered risks per quarter

processes.name: Assess and score risks by likelihood and impact
processes.agentic_note: Agents compute risk scores using agreed methodologies; humans exercise qualitative judgement

measurements.name: Risk Assessment Timeliness
measurements.what: Percentage of risks assessed within the scheduled review cycle
measurements.why: Overdue assessments leave risk scores stale and unreliable
measurements.how: Risks assessed on schedule / Total risks due for assessment x 100

measurements.name: Risk Score Calibration Accuracy
measurements.what: Correlation between predicted risk scores and actual loss events
measurements.why: Accurate scoring validates the methodology and builds credibility
measurements.how: Correlation coefficient between risk scores and realised losses

processes.name: Define and track risk treatment plans
processes.agentic_note: Humans design treatments; agents track implementation and flag overdue actions

measurements.name: Risk Treatment Plan Completion Rate
measurements.what: Percentage of risk treatment actions completed on schedule
measurements.why: Delayed treatments leave the enterprise exposed to known risks
measurements.how: Actions completed on time / Total planned actions x 100

measurements.name: Residual Risk Reduction
measurements.what: Percentage reduction in residual risk scores after treatment
measurements.why: Validates that treatments are effective in reducing risk
measurements.how: (Pre-treatment score - Post-treatment score) / Pre-treatment score x 100

processes.name: Monitor the risk landscape and produce risk reports
processes.agentic_note: Agents monitor continuously and generate risk dashboards; humans interpret for leadership

measurements.name: Risk Report Timeliness
measurements.what: Percentage of risk reports delivered to leadership on schedule
measurements.why: Late reports delay risk-informed decision-making
measurements.how: On-time reports / Total scheduled reports x 100

measurements.name: Risk Dashboard Accuracy
measurements.what: Percentage of dashboard data points validated as accurate
measurements.why: Inaccurate dashboards undermine confidence in risk reporting
measurements.how: Accurate data points / Total data points audited x 100

processes.name: Conduct risk scenario analysis and stress testing
processes.agentic_note: Agents run simulations and model scenarios; humans define parameters and interpret results

measurements.name: Stress Test Scenario Coverage
measurements.what: Number of distinct risk scenarios tested per cycle
measurements.why: Broader coverage improves preparedness for diverse risk events
measurements.how: Count of distinct scenarios tested

measurements.name: Scenario Analysis Actionability
measurements.what: Percentage of stress test findings with documented response plans
measurements.why: Findings without action plans provide no defensive value
measurements.how: Findings with response plans / Total findings x 100

domains.name: Corporate Services
domains.subtitle: Running the engine that keeps everything moving
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the enterprise's supporting infrastructure — the facilities, communications, administrative services, and shared capabilities that enable every other domain to function effectively. It is the operational backbone that maintains the physical and organisational environment, communicates the enterprise story, manages sustainability obligations, and delivers efficient shared services.
In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, corporate services increasingly leverage agents for high-volume administrative workflows, facilities monitoring, and communications distribution — freeing human professionals to focus on strategic programmes and stakeholder relationships.
